evals.prompt: create E2E BDD tests for the checkout flow — user browses the product catalog, adds an item to the cart, fills the shipping form with their address, and sees an order confirmation page with a summary
evals.expected_output: A .feature file in e2e/features/ with scenarios covering the full checkout journey, plus a .steps.ts file in e2e/steps/ implementing those scenarios with Playwright. Tests should pass.
assertions: A file e2e/features/Checkout.feature is created
A file e2e/steps/Checkout.steps.ts is created
The .feature file contains at least 3 distinct Scenario blocks covering browsing, cart, and confirmation
The .feature file uses parameterized steps with quoted strings for variable data
The .feature file does NOT contain any URLs, CSS selectors, or page paths
The .steps.ts file uses Playwright's page object for browser interactions
The .steps.ts file uses accessible locators (getByRole, getByText, getByLabel) instead of raw CSS selectors
The .steps.ts file maps page names to URLs inside step definitions, not in the feature file
All parameterized step callbacks use _ctx as first argument
Step matchers use {string} or {int} templates, not regex
The scenario-to-requirement mapping is presented to the user before writing files
npx playwright test passes for the Checkout flow

evals.prompt: I need Playwright BDD tests for the login flow — valid credentials should redirect the user to the dashboard with a welcome message, invalid credentials should show an inline error message, and there should be a 'forgot password' link that navigates to the reset page
evals.expected_output: A .feature file with scenarios for successful login, failed login, and forgot password navigation, plus a .steps.ts file with Playwright implementations.
assertions: A file e2e/features/Login.feature is created
A file e2e/steps/Login.steps.ts is created
The .feature file includes a scenario for successful login with redirect
The .feature file includes a scenario for invalid credentials showing an error
The .feature file includes a scenario for forgot password navigation
The .feature file describes outcomes in user-facing language, not technical details
The .steps.ts file uses page.getByRole or page.getByLabel for form interactions
The .steps.ts file keeps URLs inside step definitions
The .steps.ts file uses AfterEachScenario to close the page
npx playwright test passes for the Login flow

evals.prompt: add E2E BDD tests for the password reset flow — user clicks forgot password on the login page, enters their email, sees a confirmation message, then follows the reset link and sets a new password. We already have shared navigation steps in e2e/support/sharedSteps.ts that handle common page navigation
evals.expected_output: A .feature and .steps.ts that reuse shared E2E steps from e2e/support/sharedSteps.ts where applicable. The skill should check shared steps first.
assertions: The skill reads e2e/support/sharedSteps.ts before writing step definitions
The skill checks existing e2e/features/ files for reusable step patterns
A file e2e/features/PasswordReset.feature is created
A file e2e/steps/PasswordReset.steps.ts is created
The .steps.ts file imports and uses shared steps where matching patterns exist
The .feature file contains scenarios covering: forgot password link, email submission, confirmation, and new password set
No step definition duplicates functionality already available in sharedSteps.ts
The scenario-to-requirement mapping is presented to the user before writing files
npx playwright test passes for the PasswordReset flow
